title: Finite Singular-Value Shadow Checks

solver_reuse.target: finite singular-value bad spectral-norm bound QF_LRA/Farkas regression seed
solver_reuse.pressure: Exact singular-value replay computes sigma_max(A) = 3 for a fixed diagonal matrix while the malformed row claims sigma_max(A) <= 2.
solver_reuse.next_step: Promote additional singular-value rows only when they add distinct SVD, norm, rank, perturbation, or proof-route pressure; keep general SVD existence, spectral theorem, pseudospectra, and floating-point SVD algorithms in theorem or numerical-honesty lanes.

graduation_criteria: Encode the fixed rational matrix, Gram matrix, singular vectors, singular values, SVD reconstruction, spectral norm, Frobenius norm, and two-norm condition number as deterministic exact-rational replay obligations.
Route false finite singular-value upper bounds through checked QF_LRA/Farkas evidence after replay computes the exact scalar singular value.
Expose the pack through matrix computation, spectral, numerical-analysis, functional-operator, and exact-vs-floating learner and consumer query surfaces.

Keep general SVD existence, spectral theorem, perturbation theory, pseudospectra, rank-revealing algorithms, and floating-point SVD stability marked Lean-horizon or numerical-honesty until formal routes exist.
